stories.headline: Rashee Rice walks free, dodges the noose — and the saloon's still sellin' him cheap
stories.read: The Chiefs' young gun finished his 30-day stretch and rode out clean, and the wire says no suspension's comin' down the trail since the league quit hangin' fellas for a little smoke. His busted knee got cleaned up and he might not even start camp laid up. Spooked managers let him slide clear to the third round as the WR12, pardner, and that's a vein of cheap gold in Mahomes' offense — go stake your claim before the herd remembers who he is.

stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east to New England — and leaves a gold mine behind him
stories.read: Well now, the Eagles shipped their star receiver to the Patriots, and Drake Maye throws a prettier ball than the saloon gives him credit for, so Brown's a true No. 1 again on greener grass. But the real strike is back in Philly: DeVonta Smith just became the lone top dog and inherits a fatter cut of the throws for the first time since he was a greenhorn. Pan that creek — Smith's the diamond climbin' toward fringe-WR1 paydirt while folks fuss over Brown.

stories.headline: Pickens reports, swears off the holdout — the Dallas wagon rolls straight
stories.read: George Pickens trotted into mandatory minicamp and told the outfit he ain't holdin' out, set to ride 2026 on the franchise tag for $27.3 million. A happy, locked-in No. 2 across from CeeDee Lamb with Dak slingin' it means steady volume and no summer drama to spook ya. The herd's let him drift on the cheap frettin' about a holdout that ain't comin' — grab that share of the haul before his price climbs the canyon.
